name: Curry Leaves Chutney
description: Curry Leaves Chutney or Karuveppilai Chutney is a super aromatic, nutritious and tasty chutney variety made with fresh curry leaves, roasted lentils, coconut and spices. It is eaten with breakfasts, snacks and even with rice. This chutney is not only delicious but also helps in digestion, and is a wonderful way to consume the iron-rich curry leaves that are usually discarded.

steps.instruction: Rinse curry leaves in plenty of water at least thrice. Drain and pat dry with a cloth or kitchen tissues.
Heat ½ tbsp oil in a pan. Add chana dal, urad dal and cumin seeds. Fry on medium heat until light golden.
steps.tip: Chana dal is preferred for its texture and aroma, but you can replace it with roasted gram or roasted peanuts.

steps.instruction: Add green chillies and garlic (or ginger). Fry until chillies blister. Remove to a plate to cool.
To the same pan, add another ½ tbsp oil and add curry leaves. Fry on medium heat until crisp but not burnt.
Add coconut and stir fry for 1 to 2 minutes until hot. Turn off and cool completely.
steps.tip: Fresh coconut helps retain the bright green colour. Using red chillies will alter the colour.

steps.instruction: Add roasted lentils, cumin, chillies, garlic, salt and lemon juice to a grinder.
Add curry leaves and coconut. Pour ¼ cup water and begin to blend.
Add more water up to ¾ cup total, depending on desired consistency. Blend to a smooth texture.
steps.tip: Without dal or peanuts, the chutney can have a coarse texture from curry leaf fibres.

steps.instruction: Taste test and adjust salt and lemon juice. Transfer to a bowl.
Optional tempering: Heat ½ tbsp oil. Add mustard, urad dal, dried red chilli, garlic and curry leaves. When crisp, add hing and pour over chutney.
Serve with idli, dosa, uttapam, pongal, upma, pakora, parathas or chapati.
steps.tip: This chutney stays good in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 days.

tips: Chana dal is preferred for its texture and aroma, but you can replace it with roasted gram or roasted peanuts.
Without dal or peanuts, the chutney can have a coarse texture from curry leaf fibres.
Fresh coconut helps retain the bright green colour. Using red chillies will alter the colour.
Coconut can be replaced with the same amount of shallots or sliced onions — fry onions separately until golden.
You can dry curry leaves in shade for a few days until crisp, crush to powder and store for later use.
This chutney stays good in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 days.
